abstract: There is limited understanding of how paralinguistic cues relate to expressions of certainty in human-agent vs. human-human interactions. We collected data through two phases: (1) we analyzed the pronunciation of phrases from participants across different interaction scenarios and with different certainty levels to establish what cues people use to express certainty; and (2) through an online survey, we presented participants with recordings of humans and agents uttering phrases across different scenarios, and asked participants to identify the certainty level of each recording. We found that it is easier for people to express and recognize low certainty consistently, vs. high certainty, in both human-human and human-agent interactions. Additionally, people tend to rate utterances from agents with lower certainty than humans, even when using the same cues. We contribute design recommendations for agents using paralinguistic cues to express varying levels of certainty across different interaction contexts.

ur.ai_reason: 核心研究人与AI agent交互中的确定性表达，含用户实验、问卷调查用户感知
ur.tldr: 研究副语言线索（如语调、停顿）如何影响人-agent交互中的确定性表达。研究分两阶段：发音分析+问卷调查。结果发现：低确定性在表达和识别上均比高确定性更稳定一致；相比人类交互，用户倾向于给智能体更低的确定性评分，即使使用相同的语音线索。研究为智能体在交互情境中如何利用副语言线索表达不同确定性水平提供设计建议。

title: The Interplay of Paralinguistic Cues and Expressions of Certainty in Human-Human and Human-Agent Interactions
title_zh: 副语言线索与确定性表达在人际与人机交互中的相互作用
